settings.title: Email settings
settings.mesaServerDesc: Emails send through Mesa. Your address is set as Reply-To so guests can reply directly to you. No extra setup needed.
settings.ownAccountDesc: Emails send directly from your address using your SMTP credentials. Guests see your restaurant's email in the From field. Works with Gmail App Passwords, Google Workspace, or any business email.
settings.contactEmailNoteReplyTo: Shown publicly. Guests who reply will reach this address.
settings.contactEmailNoteSmtp: Shown publicly and used as the From address on outgoing emails.
settings.notificationEmailNote: Where new booking alerts are sent. Leave blank to use your contact email.
settings.gmailNote: Gmail: Google Account → Security → 2-Step Verification → App passwords
settings.reservationTimesNote: Define the specific times guests can book. If none are set, all 30-minute slots between open and close time are available.
settings.noCustomTimes: No custom times set — using open/close slot generation.

s1.p1: Mesa ("Mesa", "we", "us", or "our") operates a restaurant reservation platform that connects diners with restaurants. This Privacy Policy explains what personal data we collect, why we collect it, how we use it, and your rights over it.
s1.p2: By using Mesa, you acknowledge that your personal data will be processed as described in this Privacy Policy.

s2.intro: We collect data in three ways: data you give us, data created by your use of the service, and data restaurants share with us about you.
s2.fullNameDesc: used to identify you across the platform.
s2.emailAddrDesc: account login, email verification, and transactional notifications.
s2.passwordDesc: securely hashed before storage; never stored or transmitted in plain text.
s2.roleDesc: whether you are a diner or a restaurant owner.
s2.guestContact: Guest name, email address, and phone number (even for guests without an account).
s2.dateTime: Reservation date, time, and party size.
s2.notes: Special notes or requests you add to a reservation.
s2.statusHistory: Reservation status history (pending, confirmed, seated, cancelled, no-show, completed).
s2.reviewItem: Star rating and written review text you submit for a restaurant.
s2.guestNotesP: Restaurant staff may attach internal notes to a guest's profile. These notes are visible only to staff of that restaurant and to us as the platform operator. Restaurants are responsible for ensuring that any notes they add comply with applicable privacy laws and do not contain sensitive personal data unless legally permitted.
s2.restaurantInfo: Restaurant name, address, phone, email, cuisine type, and operating hours.
s2.smtpCreds: SMTP credentials for custom email sending (stored encrypted at rest).
s2.authTokens: Authentication tokens stored securely in your browser to maintain your session.
s2.serverLogs: Standard server logs (IP address, timestamp, request path) retained for up to 30 days.
s2.noAds: We do not use advertising or cross-site tracking cookies.

s3.p1: Mesa uses essential browser storage technologies required for authentication, session management, security, and core platform functionality. Specifically:
s3.authTokensDesc: stored securely in your browser to maintain your session.
s3.sessionStateDesc: temporary data held in memory while you use the platform.
s3.p2: We do not use advertising cookies, third-party behavioral tracking technologies, or cross-site tracking of any kind. If we add any non-essential cookies in the future, we will update this policy and obtain your consent where required by law.

s4.r2Data: Guest contact details, date/time, party size, notes
s4.r3Data: Email, name — reservation status, verification, password change alerts
s4.r4Data: Star rating, review text, user identity
s4.r7Data: Any data required by applicable law

s5.p1: We send the following transactional emails. They cannot be unsubscribed from while you have an active account, as they are necessary to operate the service:
s5.item2: Reservation confirmation, rejection, or status change.
s5.noMarketing: We do not send marketing emails. If that changes, we will ask for your explicit consent first.

s6.intro: We do not sell your personal data. We share data only in these limited cases:
s6.r1Desc: When you make a reservation or join a waitlist at a restaurant, that restaurant's staff can see your name, contact details, party size, notes, and reservation history within their venue.
s6.r2Desc: Our database and server run on cloud infrastructure. These providers process data solely on our instructions and under data processing agreements.
s6.r3Desc: If you configure a custom SMTP server, emails are routed through it. We are not responsible for that provider's privacy practices.
s6.r4Desc: We may disclose data if compelled by a valid legal order.

s7.item1Desc: retained until you delete your account.
s7.item2Desc: retained for 2 years after the reservation date to support dispute resolution, then deleted.
s7.item3Desc: retained until you delete the review or your account is deleted.
s7.item4Desc: retained for 30 days, then automatically purged.
s7.item5Desc: personal data is removed within 30 days of deletion. Anonymized aggregate data (e.g. reservation counts) may be retained indefinitely.

s8.intro: We take reasonable technical measures to protect your data:
s8.item1: Passwords are securely hashed before storage and are never stored or transmitted in plain text.
s8.item2: Authentication uses short-lived access tokens and rotating refresh tokens.
s8.item3: SMTP credentials stored encrypted at rest.
s8.note: No system is perfectly secure. If you suspect unauthorized access to your account, change your password immediately and contact us at

s9.intro: Depending on your location, you may have the following rights over your personal data. Submit requests to
s9.accessDesc: request a copy of the data we hold about you.
s9.correctionDesc: ask us to fix inaccurate data.
s9.deletionDesc: request deletion of your account and personal data (subject to legal retention obligations).
s9.portabilityDesc: receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
s9.restrictionDesc: ask us to pause processing while a dispute is resolved.
s9.objectDesc: object to processing based on legitimate interest.
s9.withdrawDesc: where processing is based on consent, withdraw it at any time without affecting prior processing.
s9.eu: EU/EEA residents: if you believe we have violated your rights under GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority.
s9.california: California residents: under CCPA you have rights to know, delete, and opt out of the sale of personal information. We do not sell personal information.

s10.p: Mesa is not directed at children under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from anyone under 16. If you believe a child has provided us data, contact
s10.pSuffix: and we will delete it promptly.

s11.p: We may update this policy. When we do, we will update the effective date and, for material changes, notify registered users by email. If required by law, we will obtain your consent for material changes before they take effect.

s12.intro: Questions or requests about this policy:
s12.name: Mesa
